narrative.headline: 2022 GMC Terrain: fuel and engine complaints dominate owner reports
narrative.summary: Owners filed 73 complaints with NHTSA. The biggest area is the Fuel/Propulsion System at 32% of reports, followed by the Fuel System at 18%, Gasoline at 16%, and the Engine at 16%, with the Power Train at 14%. Stalling and check-engine warnings show up repeatedly across these reports. No crashes, fires, injuries, or deaths were reported.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: The top complaint area: 23 reports (32%). Owners describe stalling and service-engine warnings. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported, but it is the area to check most carefully.

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 13 reports (18%). Reports center on stalling while driving with the check-engine light on. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Gasoline
issues.blurb: 12 reports (16%), again tied to stalling and check-engine warnings. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 12 reports (16%), including misfires. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Watch for any rough running or warning lights on a test drive.

issues.blurb: 10 reports (14%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Check how the car drives and shifts on a test drive.
